text:
Charlie sat on the porch steps, the sun casting warm shadows across his face. He sipped his black coffee pensively.

"Have you ever lost someone who meant everything to you?" he asked out loud.

"Not really. Why?"

A deep sigh escaped him. "Because I never understood why."

A gentle breeze rustled the leaves nearby. Charlie shook his head.

"I kept thinking about her every day. We'd sit around together. Laugh a lot. Sometimes I'd drive past that old woods just up the road… She'd call me from there. We'd plan trips—see that old barn. We'd talk about anything. Remember the Christmas lights?"

Charlie smiled wistfully. "All the things we never got to do."

His mind drifted back.

"We could've gone up there for Christmas. Her favorite time."

"No. She hated cold."

Charlie laughed lightly. "Yeah, I think you got that from her."

There was a pause. He continued, more to himself.
